title: 4. Final Test (The Climactic Performance) → Andrew, betrayed by F
content:
4. Final Test (The Climactic Performance) → Andrew, betrayed by Fletcher, could leave the stage—but he doesn’t.
• Decision: He plays the best performance of his life—not for Fletcher, but for himself.
• Consequence: In one final act of defiance, he earns Fletcher’s respect—but at what cost?

📌 McKee’s Principle Applied:
• Whiplash never tells us what kind of person Andrew is—his actions show us.
• Each scene forces him to choose between personal sacrifice or mediocrity.
• His character arc is entirely built on these painful, escalating choices.

4. The Rule of Escalation

McKee states that every scene must raise the stakes.

“A scene must turn. It must begin one way and end in another—otherwise, why is it there?”

🔥 How Whiplash Masters Escalation
Each major scene intensifies conflict:
1. Fletcher recruits Andrew → humiliates him in rehearsal.
